key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future molten-pool, wire, and keyhole geometry from past images plus process/sensor signals, enabling lookahead weld-pool segmentation for delayed closed-loop control.
engineering_relevance: This gives laser wire-feed welding controllers an earlier view of pool evolution, which can improve timing, trajectory correction, and defect avoidance under sensing and actuation delay.
method: Uses historical coaxial grayscale frames with welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals as multimodal inputs.
Applies physics-guided feature normalization, patch-level temporal state space modeling, and horizon-conditioned latent prediction to forecast future segmentation.
Adds motion-aware decoding with auxiliary SDF, temporal consistency, and keyhole-focused losses to preserve geometry and local dynamics.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing history, patch-level state space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness are most important.
watch_item: Performance depends on well-aligned multimodal sensing and may be sensitive to process changes or sensor noise outside the training distribution.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
